slides.title: Your "NetDevOps transformation" might be *Git with extra steps*.
1 — Configs in a repo, pushed by hand
slides.body:
Truth is *stored* in Git but *decided* in a CLI session.
A backup folder with a commit history.

slides.title: 2 — PRs approved unread
slides.body:
400 lines. "LGTM" in 40 seconds.
A *turnstile*, not a gate.

slides.title: 3 — A green pipeline nobody trusts
slides.body:
The checks pass — and someone SSHes in to verify anyway.
It tests what was easy, not what *fails*.

slides.title: 4 — A branch named prod-final-v2
slides.body:
Branch names are process ambiguity, written down.
Not a strategy — *archaeology*.

slides.title: 5 — Rollback = SSH
slides.body:
Forward path automated. Reverse path: a hero typing fast at 2am.
A *one-way door* at machine speed.

slides.title: Same root cause, all five
slides.body:
The tool got adopted.
The *behavior* didn't.

slides.title: Git stores your configs. It can't store your discipline.
slides.body:
A transformation is measured by what changes at 2am.

Follow *@mzsnetworks*
